methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $123M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 6 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $10.0B.

private.name: 1X Technologies
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $10B

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $350M

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Neura Robotics is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by David Reger, currently valued at €2.6B. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine comparable companies: one public (277810.KQ) and eight private firms. The private peer set includes 1X Technologies ($10.0B), Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), and Fairmat ($350M), among others.

Two triangulation methods are applied. Stage-progression analysis, using total raised ($123.3M) as a proxy base value and applying a 1.9x Series B step-up multiplier, implies a valuation of $234M. Peer-median analysis across six same-sector, same-stage comparables yields a median valuation of $1.1B, with a range spanning $300M to $10.0B. The wide peer range reflects meaningful dispersion in the robotics sector at this stage.

Synthesizing both methods produces a triangulated range of $199M (low) to $1.2B (high), with a median of $655M. The current €2.6B valuation sits materially above this analytically supported range. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion within the peer set—particularly the $10B outlier (1X Technologies)—and limited public comparables. The stage-progression method may underweight capital efficiency or market positioning relative to peers.
